test: High-sensitivity cardiac troponin I or T (hs-cTn) measured at presentation (0h) and 1 hour later (1h), interpreted via the ESC 0/1-hour algorithm: rule-out if 0h concentration is below the assay-specific limit-of-detection (e.g. <5 ng/L Roche hs-cTnT, <6 ng/L Abbott hs-cTnI) AND 0/1h absolute change is below the assay-specific delta threshold; rule-in if 0h concentration is at or above the assay-specific high cutoff OR 0/1h delta exceeds the assay-specific delta threshold; observe-zone otherwise.
reference_standard: Final adjudicated diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction (Type 1 or Type 2) per the Universal Definition of Myocardial Infarction, by two or more independent cardiologists blinded to hs-cTn concentrations, using all available clinical, biochemical, ECG, imaging, and follow-up data including 30-day or longer MACE follow-up.
target_condition: Acute myocardial infarction (NSTEMI; STEMI excluded by ECG before troponin sampling).
population: Adults presenting to an emergency department with chest pain or other symptoms suggestive of acute coronary syndrome, in whom STEMI has been excluded by ECG and a troponin sample is drawn within the relevant clinical-onset window. Excludes terminal renal failure on chronic dialysis (variable handling across studies), known active myocarditis, and patients with overt non-cardiac terminal illness in some cohorts.
verification_protocol: Every PMID + DOI in this dataset was verified via PubMed lookup_article_by_citation + get_article_metadata on 2026-04-29. Per lessons.md 2026-04-28 LLM-citation-misattribution rule and the T35 D-dimer audit (which found ~62% of D-dimer DOIs misattributed in the prior LLM-drafted dataset), no citation is recorded here without a Crossref/PubMed metadata-match against the in-page citation label.

primary_tier.delta_threshold: 0/1h absolute delta thresholds derived in derivation cohort and applied prospectively to validation cohort
primary_tier.raw_quote: We prospectively enrolled 1,500 patients presenting with suspected AMI to the emergency department. The hs-cTnI (Siemens Vista) 0-/1-hour algorithm incorporated measurements performed at baseline and absolute changes within 1 hour, was derived in the first 750 patients (derivation cohort), and then validated in the second 750 (validation cohort). Overall, AMI was the final diagnosis in 16% of patients. Applying the algorithm in the validation cohort, 57% of patients could be classified as rule-out; 10%, as rule-in; and 33%, as observe. Sensitivity and negative predictive value for AMI in the rule-out zone were 100% (95% CI 96-100) and 100% (95% CI 99-100), respectively. Specificity and positive predictive value for AMI in the rule-in zone were 96% (95% CI 94-97) and 70% (95% CI 60-79), respectively.

primary_tier.delta_threshold: absolute 0/2h delta thresholds (rule-out and rule-in) derived in derivation cohort and applied prospectively to validation cohort
primary_tier.raw_quote: We prospectively enrolled 1148 (derivation cohort) and 517 (external validation cohort) unselected patients presenting with suspected AMI to the emergency department. Final diagnosis was adjudicated by 2 independent cardiologists. Hs-cTnT was measured at presentation and after 2 hours. AMI was the final diagnosis in 16% of patients in the derivation and 9.1% in the validation cohort. Applying the 2-hour triage algorithm in the external validation cohort, 78% of patients could be classified as rule-out, 8% as rule-in, and 14% in the observational-zone. Resulting sensitivity and NPV were 96% and 99.5%, respectively, for rule-out, and specificity and PPV were 99% and 85%, respectively, for rule-in.

primary_tier.delta_threshold: ESC-recommended 0/1h delta thresholds (rule-out and rule-in) for hs-cTnI Abbott applied prospectively
primary_tier.raw_quote: Four strategies for very early rule-out of acute myocardial infarction using high-sensitivity cardiac troponin I (hs-cTnI) have been identified... [APACE prospective cohort; numbers extracted from Table 2 of Boeddinghaus 2017 Circulation; 2x2 cells back-computed from reported Sens/Spec/N and AMI prevalence in the validation analysis of the ESC 0/1h algorithm arm].

primary_tier.test_brand: Roche Elecsys hs-cTnT or Abbott ARCHITECT hs-cTnI per local lab
primary_tier.delta_threshold: ESC 0/1h algorithm applied per ESC 2015 / 2020 guidelines
primary_tier.raw_quote: Outcome of Applying the ESC 0/1-hour Algorithm in Patients With Suspected Myocardial Infarction. Numbers extracted from the published Table reporting Sens/Spec/NPV/PPV against adjudicated final diagnosis with 30-day MACE follow-up; 2x2 back-computed from reported NPV and Sens applied to the rule-out arm.

primary_tier.delta_threshold: 0/1h hs-cTnT protocol with rule-out at limit-of-detection (<5 ng/L) and standard delta thresholds
primary_tier.raw_quote: Among patients discharged from the emergency department, the 0/1-hour protocol had a negative predictive value of 99.6% (95% CI 99.0-99.9%) for 30-day death or myocardial infarction. The 0/1-hour hs-cTnT protocol arm comprised 1646 patients; 17/1646 (1.0%) had subsequent 30-day MI or death. 45.1% of the 0/1h arm were discharged from ED.
